executive_board.name: Matthew Boyle
executive_board.bio: Matthew Boyle is a junior Finance major in the Smeal College of Business from Ellicott City, MD. He currently serves as the President in the Nittany Lion Fund after previously serving as a Director of NLF Education. This summer, Matthew will be interning at Evercore in the Investment Banking Division. In his free time, Matthew enjoys golfing, cooking, and hanging out with friends and family. He says that the Nittany Lion Fund has helped him strengthen his analytical skills and ability to perform under pressure.

leadership.name: Jared Hollister
leadership.bio: Jared Hollister is a freshman Finance and Math major in the Smeal College of Business and Eberly College of Science from Syracuse, NY. He currently serves as an Associate Analyst of Information Technology and a Director of Monthly Reports in the Nittany Lion Fund. After spending one semester in the Penn State Investment Association, he has developed an interest in investment banking. In his free time, Jared enjoys playing basketball, lifting weights, and golfing. He says that the Nittany Lion Fund has helped him develop strong attention to detail and time management skills.

leadership.name: Neev Dani
leadership.bio: Neev Dani is a freshman Finance major in the Smeal College of Business from Virginia Beach, VA. He currently serves as an Associate Analyst of the Information Technology Sector and a Director of Monthly Reports in the Nittany Lion Fund. After spending two semesters in the Penn State Investment Association, he has developed an interest in investment banking. In his free time, Neev enjoys golfing, traveling, and watching sports. He says that the Nittany Lion Fund has helped him develop a strong attention to detail as well as communication skills.

leadership.name: Michael Tunder
leadership.bio: Michael Tunder is a freshman Finance and Accounting major in the Smeal College of Business from Pittsburgh, PA. He currently serves as an Associate Analyst of the Consumer Discretionary Sector in the Nittany Lion Fund. After spending one semester in the Penn State Investment Association, he has developed an interest in investment banking. In his free time, Michael enjoys playing volleyball, watching hockey, and hiking. He says that the Nittany Lion Fund has helped him become a more effective communicator and has also helped him develop his time-management skills.

leadership.name: Tommy Flanagan
leadership.bio: Thomas (Tommy) Flanagan is a freshman Finance major in the Smeal College of Business from Weston, MA. He currently serves as the Associate Fund Manager of the Energy Sector and a Director of Quarterly Reports in the Nittany Lion Fund. After spending one semester in the Penn State Investment Association, he has developed an interest in investment banking. In his free time, Tommy enjoys lacrosse, Formula 1, and hockey. He says that the Nittany Lion Fund has taught him how to manage his time efficiently and hold himself accountable to deadlines, helping him complete deliverables effectively and on time.

leadership.name: Zack Limkakeng
leadership.bio: Ishmael (Zack) Limkakeng is a freshman Finance major in the Smeal College of Business from Pleasanton, CA. He currently serves as the Associate Analyst of Utilities in the Nittany Lion Fund. After spending one semester in the Penn State Investment Association, he has developed an interest in investment banking. In his free time, Zack enjoys playing basketball, skiing, and traveling. He says that the Nittany Lion Fund has helped him develop strong attention to detail and effective time management skills.

leadership.name: Mary Winand
leadership.bio: Mary Winand is a freshman Math major in the Eberly College of Science from Lancaster, PA. She currently serves as the Associate Analyst of the Consumer Staples Sector and a Director of Outreach in the Nittany Lion Fund. After spending one semester in the Penn State Investment Association, she has developed an interest in quantitative finance and investment banking. In her free time, Mary enjoys running, board games, and watching Survivor. She says that the Nittany Lion Fund has taught her that there is always more you can do to improve yourself and your work.
